steps.title: Identify the research finding about optimal volume
steps.content: The decisive signal is “optimal volume tended to be higher for the musician than for the nonmusician” when both identified the same recording as their favorite. This comparison establishes what we'd expect for the nonmusician's optimal volume relative to 84.8 dB.

steps.title: Apply the finding to the scenario
steps.content:
At 84.8 dB, the nonmusician is above their optimal point and would find the recording more enjoyable at a lower volume.

The other three choices either reference different recordings or imply zero enjoyment—none follow from the finding about optimal volumes for the shared favorite recording.

That matches the correct answer — D is correct.
